channel.title: Hospitalization on Tokyo Police Hospital
channel.description: Recent content in Hospitalization on Tokyo Police Hospital

item.title: Admission & Discharge
item.description:
Admission & Discharge Support Center

Our Admission & Discharge Support Center coordinates your hospital stay from the moment admission is scheduled until you are safely discharged and connected to appropriate community care.

Phone: 03-5343-5611 (main line) → ask for Admission & Discharge Support Center

Admission Process

Step 1: Outpatient Consultation

Your attending physician will determine whether hospitalization is necessary and schedule your admission date.

Step 2: Admission Booking

The Admission & Discharge Support Center will:

item.title: Room Types & Fees
item.description:
Room Types and Fees

Tokyo Police Hospital offers a range of room types to suit different needs and preferences. Rooms covered by standard health insurance have no extra room charge. Private rooms carry an additional daily fee, which is not covered by health insurance and must be paid out-of-pocket.

Room Fee Table

Room Type Daily Fee (¥, tax incl.) Size (㎡) Number of Rooms Facilities
4-bed Room Free 32 ㎡ (8㎡/bed) 64 rooms Standard
Private Room A ¥20,900 17 ㎡ 24 rooms Shower & Toilet
Private Room B ¥19,800 18 ㎡ 2 rooms Shower & Toilet
Private Room C ¥18,150 16 ㎡ 55 rooms Toilet only
Private Room D ¥14,630 15 ㎡ 45 rooms No shower/toilet
Special Suite (Standard) ¥37,400 29 ㎡ — Shower & Toilet
Special Suite (Deluxe) ¥52,800 43 ㎡ — Shower & Toilet

All fees are per night and include tax. Room fees are not covered by public health insurance and are charged in addition to your medical expenses.

item.title: Daily Life During Hospitalization
item.description:
Daily Routine

During your stay, each day will generally follow this schedule:

Time Activity
7:45 Breakfast served
12:00 Lunch served
18:00 Dinner served
Morning Doctor’s rounds
Throughout the day Nursing care, therapy sessions, tests
21:00 Lights out (please rest quietly after this time)
22:00 Ward quieted; visitor departures required

Meals

Hospital meals are planned by our registered dietitians and tailored to your medical condition and dietary needs. Meals are served three times a day. If you have food allergies, religious dietary requirements, or other concerns, please inform the dietitian or nursing staff.

item.title: Visiting Rules
item.description:
Visiting Hours
Visiting Hours 15:00 – 19:00 (daily, including weekends and holidays)
Maximum Visit Duration 1 hour per visit
Maximum Number of Visitors Up to 3 visitors at one time
Minimum Age for Visitors 12 years old and above

We appreciate your understanding and cooperation in following these rules to protect the health and recovery of all patients.

General Rules for Visitors

  1. Masks are required at all times within the hospital building.
  2. Hand hygiene – please use the hand sanitizer provided at the ward entrance before and after visiting.
  3. No food or beverages may be brought into patient rooms unless specifically approved by the attending physician or dietitian.
  4. Please speak quietly and be mindful of other patients resting nearby.
  5. Do not enter other patients’ rooms.
  6. Photography and video recording of other patients or staff is strictly prohibited.

Restrictions on Cut Flowers

To protect patients with compromised immune systems, the following restrictions apply:

item.title: Get Well Email Service
item.description:
Get Well Email Service

Can’t visit in person? Our Get Well Email Service allows family, friends, and colleagues to send warm messages and encouragement to patients admitted at Tokyo Police Hospital.

Messages are received by the hospital, printed, and delivered directly to the patient’s bedside.

How to Use the Service

  1. Compose your message via the dedicated email service form or address.
  2. Include the following information in your message:
    • Patient’s full name
    • Ward name and room number (if known)
    • Your name and relationship to the patient
    • Your message
  3. Send your email to the address provided by hospital staff or on the hospital’s Japanese website.
  4. Hospital staff will print and deliver your message to the patient, typically within 1–2 business days.

Guidelines for Messages

  • Please write in Japanese or English.
  • Keep messages appropriate and positive — this service is meant to encourage patients.
  • Do not include sensitive personal or financial information.
  • The hospital reserves the right to withhold messages that are deemed inappropriate or that may affect the patient’s recovery.

Patient Privacy

The hospital will handle all messages in accordance with our privacy policy. Messages will only be delivered to the named patient. We will not share personal information with third parties.

item.title: DPC Payment System
item.description:
What Is the DPC Payment System?

Tokyo Police Hospital uses the Diagnosis Procedure Combination / Per-Diem Payment System (DPC/PDPS) to calculate inpatient medical fees. This system is applied at designated hospitals throughout Japan.

How DPC Works

Under the DPC system, your daily hospitalization cost is calculated using a standardized rate based on:

  1. Your primary diagnosis (classified using the DPC code system)
  2. Your treatment procedures (surgery, specific therapies, etc.)
  3. The length of your stay

Rather than billing individually for each examination, injection, or procedure, DPC consolidates most routine inpatient costs into a single daily package rate. This makes the total cost more predictable and often more affordable than fee-for-service billing.
